title: Solid peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid generation compositions and the use thereof
abstract: Peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acid forming compositions and methods for forming peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acids, preferably in situ, using the peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acid forming compositions are disclosed. Methods of using the peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acids, including for treating a surface or a target in need of antimicrobial or sanitizing treatment are also disclosed. Particular applications of using odor-free, low volatility peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acid sanitizers for direct food contact are disclosed.
claims:
Claims The claimed invention is: 1. A method for forming a peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid from a peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid forming composition comprising: a contacting step that is either: (a) contacting a solid peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid forming composition with a liquid, wherein the solid composition comprises a diester of lactone of an alphahydroxycarboxylic acid, an alkalinity source and/or a substance that generates hydrogen peroxide when in contact with the liquid, and forming a liquid that comprises the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid and has a pH below about 8 within about 5 minutes after the contacting of the solid composition and the liquid; or (b) contacting a liquid peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid forming composition comprising a diester of lactone of an alphahydroxy carboxylic acid in solution with a solvent and an alkalinity source and/or hydrogen peroxide, and forming a liquid that comprises the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid and has a pH below about 8 within about 5 minutes after the contacting of the solution with the alkalinity source and/or hydrogen peroxide; and forming at least about 1 ppm of the peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid within less than 1 minute of the contacting step. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the diester of lactone has the following structure 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the alphahydroxy carboxylic acid is lactic acid or glycolic acid, and wherein the diester of lactone of the alphahydroxycarboxylic acid is correspondingly lactide or glycolide. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the alkalinity source comprises an alkali metal carbonate, alkali metal percarbonate, or an alkali metal borate. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the substance that generates hydrogen peroxide comprises at least one of an alkali metal percarbonate, alkali metal perborate, carbamide peroxide or peroxide salts. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the substance that generates hydrogen peroxide is sodium percarbonate, sodium perborate, calcium peroxide, magnesium peroxide, PVP peroxide, or urea peroxide. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the alkalinity source and the substance that generates hydrogen peroxide is a single reagent in the solid composition. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the single reagent is sodium percarbonate. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the reagents are contacted to form a liquid solution comprising the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid in amounts from about 0.1 ppm to about 100,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 90,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 80,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 70,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 

60,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 50,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 40,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 30,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 20,000 ppm, or from about 0.1 ppm to about 10,000 ppm, and/or wherein the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid has less than about 1 KPa, less than about 0.5 KPa, or less than about 0.01 KPa vapor pressure at 20Â°C. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the reagents are contacted to form a liquid solution comprising from about 10 ppm to about 50,000 ppm, from about 10 ppm to about 30,000 ppm, or from about 10 ppm to about 20,000 ppm of the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the liquid that comprises the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid comprises at least about 10 ppm, at least about 100 ppm, or at least about 1,000 ppm peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid within about 1 minute of the contacting of the solid composition and the liquid. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the liquid that comprises the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid comprises at least about 100 ppm, at least about 1,000 ppm, or at least about 10,000 ppm peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid within about 5 minutes of the contacting of the solid composition and the liquid. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the reagents are contacted under ambient conditions, or at a temperature ranging from about 4Â°C to about 60Â°C, and at a pH range between about 6 to about 12, or between about 7 to about 11. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the formed liquid that comprises the 106 

peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid has a pH below about 7 within about 5 minutes after the contacting step. 15. The method of any of claims 1-14, wherein the contacting step generates the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid in situ for an application of use of the formed peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid forming composition further comprises a stabilizing agent, disintegrating reagent, dispersant and/or a pH buffering agent. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid forming composition further comprises a catalyst or an enzyme that catalyzes formation of the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid, or wherein the contacting step further includes contacting a catalyst or an enzyme that catalyzes formation of the peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid with the reagents . 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the catalyst or enzyme comprises a perhydrolytic enzyme, lipase, coronase, termanyl or esperase. 19. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein neither the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid forming composition nor the contacting step includes an enzyme and/or catalyst. 20. The method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein none of the 107 

peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid forming composition, the contacting step, nor the formed peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid include a stabilizing agent. 21. The method of any one of claims 1-20, which comprises contacting the liquid with multiple solid compositions sequentially. 22. The method of any one of claims 1-21, wherein an additional C1-C22 peroxy carboxylic acid is formed in the liquid comprising the peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid, or wherein a preformed C1-C22 peroxy carboxylic acid composition is added to the liquid comprising the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein an ester precursor and/or a carboxylic acid precursor of the additional C1-C22 peroxy carboxylic acid is included in the solid composition and/or added to the liquid comprising the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid to react and generate the additional C1-C22 peroxy carboxylic acid. 24. A peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid forming composition comprising: a) a diester of lactone of an alphahydroxycarboxylic acid, b) an alkalinity source and/or a substance that generates hydrogen peroxide when in contact with a liquid; and c) optionally an additional functional ingredient; wherein the composition is a liquid when the diester of lactone and the alphahydroxy carboxylic acid is in solution with a solvent, and wherein the composition reacts to form the 108 

 peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid having a pH below about 8 within about 5 minutes after the diester of lactone of the alphahydroxy carboxylic acid and the alkalinity source and/or substance that generates hydrogen peroxide are contacted; or wherein the composition is a solid, and wherein the solid composition reacts when dissolved in a liquid to form the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid having a pH below about 8 within about 5 minutes after the diester of lactone of the alphahydroxycarboxylic acid and the alkalinity source and/or substance that generates hydrogen peroxide are contacted. 25. The composition of claim 24, wherein the diester of lactone has the following structure wherein R is H, CH3 or an alkyl group. 26. The composition of claim 25, wherein the alphahydroxycarboxylic acid is lactic acid or glycolic acid, and wherein the diester of lactone of the alphahydroxy carboxylic acid is correspondingly lactide or glycolide. 27. The composition of any one of claims 24-26, wherein the alkalinity source comprises an alkali metal carbonate or percarbonate, an alkali metal borate, or a urea peroxide. 28. The composition of any one of claims 24-27, wherein the substance that generates hydrogen peroxide comprises at least one of an alkali metal percarbonate, alkali metal perborate, or peroxide. 29. The composition of claim 28, wherein the substance that generates hydrogen peroxide is 109 

sodium percarbonate, sodium perborate, calcium peroxide, magnesium peroxide, PVP peroxide, or urea peroxide. 30. The composition of any one of claims 24-26, wherein the alkalinity source and the substance that generates hydrogen peroxide is a single reagent in the solid composition. 31. The composition of claim 30, wherein the single reagent is sodium percarbonate. 32. The composition of any one of claims 24-31, further comprising a stabilizing agent, disintegrating reagent, a dispersant and/or a pH buffering agent. 33. The composition of claim 32, further comprising citric acid. 34. The composition of any one of claims 24-33, further comprising an activator or an enzyme. 35. The composition of claim 34, wherein the activator or enzyme comprises a perhydrolytic enzyme, lipase, coronase, termanyl or esperase. 36. The composition of any one of claims 24-35, wherein the composition is a solid, and wherein the solid is a powder, tablet, pellet or other solid forms, and optionally wherein the powders or other solid is housed in a sachet or pouch or a PVA dose package. 37. The composition of claim 36, wherein the solid is dimensionally stable for at least one year at room temperature. 110 38. A peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid composition formed by combining the solid composition of any one of claims 36-37 with water or a non-water liquid. 39. The composition of claim 38, wherein the composition is a liquid solution comprising the peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid in amounts from about 0.1 ppm to about 100,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 90,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 80,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 70,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 60,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 50,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 40,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 30,000 ppm, from about 0.1 ppm to about 20,000 ppm, or from about 0.1 ppm to about 10,000 ppm, and/or wherein the peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acid has less than about 1 KPa, less than about 0.5 KPa, or less than about 0.01 KPa vapor pressure at 20Â°C. 40. A method for treating a target, which method comprises contacting a target with an effective amount of peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid according to any one of claims 24-39 to form a treated target composition, wherein said treated target composition comprises from about 0.1 ppm to about 10,000 ppm of said peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid, and said contacting lasts for sufficient time to bleach, remove soils, disinfect and/or stabilize or reduce microbial population in and/or on said target or said treated target composition. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein the target is a surface in need of high level disinfecting and the method comprises providing an effective amount of the peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acid to the surface in need of treatment, wherein said treated surface source comprises from about 1 ppm to about 1,000 ppm of said peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acid. 42. The method of any one of claims 40-41, wherein the contacting step lasts for about 111 

1 second to about 10 minutes. 43. The method of any one of claims 40-42, wherein the contacting step is conducted at a temperature ranging from about 0Â°C to about 70Â°C. 44. The method of any one of claims 40-43, wherein the target is a food item or a plant item and/or at least a portion of a medium, a container, an equipment, a system or a facility for growing, holding, processing, packaging, storing, transporting, preparing, cooking or serving the food item or the plant item. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the treatment does not induce an organoleptic effect in and/or on the food item or a plant item. 46. The method of any one of claims 40-44, wherein the target is at least a portion of a medium, a container, an equipment, a system or a facility for producing, holding, processing, packaging, storing, or transporting pulp, and is used in papermaking, textiles, food, or pharmaceutical industry. 47. The method of any one of claims 40-43, wherein the surface is a medical instrument in need of repurposing or reprocessing. 48. The method of any one of claims 40-43, wherein the target is a water source and/or a biofilm. 49. The method of any one of claims 40-43, wherein the target is at least a portion of a 112 

medium, a surface, a container, an equipment, or a system in a health care facility. 50. The method of any one of claims 40-43, wherein the surface is a laundry article. 51. The method of claim 50, wherein the laundry article is soiled and contacted by the peroxyhydroxycarboxylic acid in a conventional laundry application, a pre-wash step, a prebleach step and/or outside of a washing machine. 52. The method of any one of claims 50-51, further comprising cleaning or laundering the laundry article with a conventional aqueous detergent, and/or further comprising a bleaching step, one or more rinse steps, a souring step and/or a softening step. 53. The method of any one of claims 40-52, wherein the peroxyalphahydroxy carboxylic acid is used at a concentration from about 1 ppm to about 10,000 ppm, from about 1 ppm to about 5,000 ppm, from about 1 ppm to about 2,000 ppm, or from about 1 ppm to about 1,000 ppm. 54. The method of any one of claims 40-53, wherein the peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid is applied to the target by means of a spray, a fog, or a foam, or by dipping all or part of the target is dipped in a composition comprising the peroxyalphahydroxycarboxylic acid. 55. The method of any one of claims 40-54, wherein the contacting step lasts for at least 5 seconds. 56. The method of any one of claims 40-55, wherein the microbial population in and/or 113 

on the target or the treated target composition is reduced by at least 50%, 90%, 99%, or 99.9%.
